Hi, welcome to video number one in the celebrity branding blueprint. My name is Greg Rowlett and throughout this program I'm joined by Emmy Award winning director and best selling author Nick Nanton. How are you doing my man? You're very excited to do this. Yep that's the stuff we've actually done for them and then through our media placements and everything we've reached millions of people with our concepts and we've certainly gotten a lot of great feedback that they work. We know they work, they're living proof that they work, we are living proof that they work and we've built a multi-million dollar business on the back of these concepts so if you just pay attention and try to implement this stuff you know you may not get to everything, you may not implement all 100 things you talk about today but if you implement 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 you will see a huge shift in your business and a huge change because this is really the secret sauce of all the people who we work with all around the world some of the biggest names in the business. Definitely so let me set the stage for what you guys are going through here in this program in this video we're going to give you an overview of what celebrity branding is all about and share with you two of our most fundamental processes and systems that you have to understand as you go through this program and then over the next five videos we're going to break down each of the five different steps in the celebrity branding blueprint. So I want to get started by talking about just this concept of both building a celebrity brand and then also one of the other terms that we throw around a lot a celebrity expert. Now a lot of people have that connotation of a celebrity. Well I don't want to be Kim Kardashian, but that's not what this is all about right? Not at all. If you have the ability to make money by not knowing anything I highly go for it. It's like winning the lotto though that money usually doesn't last long most people cannot build an empire. Most of the empires that have built an entertainment let's say the Kardashians there's an awful lot of intelligent work going on behind the scenes. But Kim is famous for kind of being famous and some other things we're not going to talk about on camera but that's just our deal. But the thing is yeah there's two types of people there's celebrities, there's experts and then there's celebrity experts so ultimately three I guess. So a celebrity somebody who's famous now the word celebrity means famous or highly recognized right? And so that's like a daunting thing to most people but there's a lot of systematic ways you can become a celebrity and we'll talk about that when it's really you're only trying to appeal you don't have to be a celebrity to millions of people. Even if you're a celebrity to a thousand people who want to pay you money that's a celebrity. Okay so celebrity then there's experts. Experts are the top notch knowledge bearers of what their industry is right? That was an interesting phrase but we'll use it. The top notch knowledge bearers in their industry and then people who know everything now the problem with a lot of experts is they end up in basements of universities they end up like curing cancer but nobody knows who they are. Right. I mean maybe in a very small community through medical papers or white papers whatever it is but an expert is somebody who really knows their field well. Now they usually don't get paid well celebrities usually get paid well we're not sure what they know but they're able to add their name their face their brand to things and they get paid for it. It's a great great plan. Right. Experts we should have no idea who they are and they don't get paid very well but they're great at what they do. So the problem is many business people become experts but it doesn't help them out because they're an expert at they're like the absolute best at this thing but they don't know how to promote that. Right. So what we really want to do is we want to reach kind of apex of this celebrity expert triangle and become the celebrity expert at the top. The celebrity expert is a person who not only is awesome at what they do everyone knows who they are and they can command top dollar because they're celebrity. If you've ever worked with a celebrity I don't know if you have but if you ever have you have to pay the rate they request. Right. So ins out ifs and buts and the hotter they are at the moment the harder it is to actually get them there. They actually start selecting whether they want to take your money or not. Right. That's a great place to be for a business person. You want to be the person that everybody wants to work with and will pay whatever the rate is. When we talk about price I think we should say something about this for a second. There's no reason to charge billions of dollars just because you want to charge billions of dollars but you've got to start creating a position and use charge the rate that you deserve. And we want to help people charge that rate because when you do that you really can work less and make more and spend the time doing things you want to be doing outside of your career but also changing the most people's lives. So that's really kind of where it all comes together for me. Definitely love that. So make sure you guys understand what the celebrity is what the expert is and then the top of that triangle the celebrity expert which is really where you're we're trying to take you right here in this program and the program is called the celebrity branding blueprint. So obviously based on the best selling book celebrity branding you. So you know what is a celebrity brand. And again from the business sense now why would we want to get to that level of having a celebrity brand. Sure. So a celebrity brand we are talking about celebrity. Let's talk about branding for a second because branding is one of the most confused things I think in the in the world the terms that's thrown around. People try to confuse people what it means. I've always said this your brand is just your story. That's it like simple. If you can just think of your brand when you hear the word brand just substitute story and branding is just storytelling. So celebrity brand is taking well there's two types of celebrity brand of course there's Britney Spears perfume celebrity brand perfume. We're not talking about that. No we're talking about how to become a celebrity in business right and build a brand. So the reason why you'd want to be a celebrity brand in your business is basically taking a personal brand what you stand for who you are what you do how you help people and putting that out in front of your business to tell the story. So you essentially are the chief storyteller. The story is told through your perspective or about you. And so the coolest part about that is that that is the only place people can never compete with you right because there's only one you if and there's ways to leverage that we'll get to talk about too because you can't do everything. But the fact of the matter is that if you become the best known person for this one thing people can try to knock you off all they want. But the end of the day if you built your celebrity brand then the only way they can get what you have is to go through you. And again that allows you to set your own terms your own price everything else. One of the things that I love the analogy to talk about it's like if you are a real estate agent just as an easy example and you've branded yourself the right way you follow these principles. Let's say six months from after reading this course you're doing it all right. The good news is that you're going to get that call on Saturday at 10 a.m. and you're at your kids baseball game. And you know what you're going to be able to say you know there's times when you have emergencies don't get me wrong. You're going to be able to say you know I'll call them back maybe later today maybe tomorrow perhaps Monday whatever you whatever the expectations are that you set up. Yeah. When you've created your celebrity brand correctly it allows you number one to set the expectation of the client of what they're going to get and how they can communicate with you. We've talked many times in many of our podcasts everything else about one of our mentors Dan Kennedy right the guy who only communicates by facts does not have a cell phone you cannot call his phone unless you have a set appointment. Right. You have to pay thousands of dollars to fly to see him in Cleveland Ohio right because he has horses and that's where he races but he sets up this expectation of you're not going to get me quickly. Right. If you want to reach me you have to fax me and then the fact is actually get FedEx to him and then he fedex is back to response to his assistant and she'll fax it back but he sets up the expectation but the reason you're willing to deal with that is the fact that you know what he has to offer he has to offer what nobody else can offer you and you want to pay the price for it and you want to be trained to communicate the way he wants to communicate. So my only point with that like the real estate agent if you have not branded yourself correctly you're constantly running at everyone else's pace worried about whether they're just going to call somebody else and I'm going to lose the deal. Well at the end of the day if you set the expectation and you have a reason why they want to work with you I don't condone at all by the way lack of responsiveness. I want to see you blow off this. Right. You'll see if you email me I respond quickly because I like to respond at least within the day I'll respond but the fact of the matter is when you build your celebrity expert status and you become a celebrity brand you get to dictate life on your terms which is good for everybody. Yeah. And then you also your clients will be receptive to the fact that you want to work this way and you're not always constantly worried about where's the next deal going to come from but even more than where am I going to lose this next deal if I'm not at every you know a beck and call of a client right. Right. Right. Definitely. So I really like that definition of celebrity brand and again it's not confusing branding with maybe what you see from Burger King or Doritos or whatever it is but even guys like Dan Kennedy have a brand he's got the no BS he's got different things around it and he's kind of built this celebrity brand around who he is. Right. And one of the things that I think is important to note here is so Dan Kennedy we do a lot of work with Dan and Dan is kind of the direct marketing you know guy he's the guy who kind of out shines everyone else in that field and Dan a lot of people in the on planet Dan's we'll call her in the direct marketing world say that Brandy is just garbage and what I think is important to note is even Dan Kennedy make sure that you understand the fact that Brandy is not garbage branding without direct response so like brand advertising like have a coconut smile you know all that sort of stuff look I'm sure there's some research that Coke can watch to see what's adjusting their sales and at the point when you're spending billions of dollars in advertising or whatever they spend you're trying everything you can find the problem is that's what most of us see the reason why we can repeat things like that or save water drink beer or you know it's the most reliable whatever it is whatever someone's big slow and tag on not in your hand all that stuff because of repetition if you have the money to give repetition about your brand in the marketplace on cable TV and billboards and all that stuff well it might work for you but most small to medium sized businesses cannot compete on that scale. Right. What I thought Dan Kennedy said is that what I loved about what he said was the fact that he said look direct marketing without branding is foolish but branding without direct marketing is like suicide it's like so if you're going to build a brand without asking for a direct response out of it then you're you're going to lose because you just don't have enough money you're not making people get off their couch and call the number or whatever it is you need to you need to ask for a response or else you never know what's working either I never know which piece is working but if you're going to go ahead and do direct response you might as well ride along with the brand because you will get people to recognize the brand you will get people to see it and so it would be foolish not to go ahead and take advantage of that opportunity but branding in and of itself is not an evil thing well that's that would be like saying storytelling is a bad idea. Right. Well I think every marketer out there whether they're a direct response guy or a big brand advertiser would say stories where it's at right. Right. Definitely. So in the rest of this video we're going to talk a lot about media strategies because one of the biggest fundamentals I think is the difference between those who are not really seen as a celebrity and those who are is is media really that's really what they have that the regular business owner doesn't right. Right. Absolutely. So media is just a medium for telling your story. Right. That's what it is. And so when we send out what we call kind of a shotgun box that's a Dan Kennedy term again out to a client the fact of the matter is when someone requests information you want to be able to send them out something that's killer you want to be able to send them out you know if they call if you're a lawyer and someone's calling around checking pricing they call three different law firms right when they call the three different law firms what's going to happen is they're going to get maybe some PDF sent to them they might get an appointment booked or whatever. What you want to be is you want to be that law firm that FedEx is a box to this good client you want to be able to afford to send that FedEx box by the way because one of the things that's common sense but not common knowledge if you hear it this way is the most successful businesses if you want to be the most successful person in your marketplace the best thing you can do is to build your business so that you can afford to spend more money to get a lead and a close conversion a client than anyone else in your marketplace. Now most people go wait a second that makes that's like hard to understand like I should be wanting to spend money to acquire clients. Yes if you can outspend your competition get a client they will always lose. Right. So if you build your business based on being able to spend money to get those clients you win. So this sort of box one of the things that we do is we create all the stuff that goes inside a shock and awe box. Right. A shock and awe box is that box if you wanted to make a connection with someone in your marketplace if you got in front of more people you're not going to close everybody but you're going to get more deals right. Yeah. So the shock and awe box is that box you can send to these people and they will call you back. Totally. We have used this concept to get celebrities to call us back Steve Forbes I mean Jack Canfield all this stuff but the box what we do for our clients at the agency is we put the media they need to have an outbox to get the call back it can be newspaper clippings from articles they've been quoted in it can be bestselling books it can be movies we've done created documentaries about them that have aired on our series on biography it can be all those things brochures pamphlets whatever it is but in a way that is all based on media and that media in different ways is telling their story. Definitely. And what I love about all these different media pieces is that's where celebrities are seen in movies celebrities are seen in the newspaper celebrities are seen in their own magazine and that's what we've created for them and that is the media and that's one of the biggest differentials and so I want to talk about our two core concepts that everyone needs to understand going through this program and the first is this concept of the business trifecta right and I love the story that you have to set up the business trifecta but this is really important because a lot of people are really are missing pieces in the trifecta. Absolutely so one of the reasons why people don't think media or marketing or PR work is because they don't understand how they fit together right. So I like to call it the business trifecta and the story I tell is kind of like when I was 16 I bought my first car it was a Daihatsu charade it was a hatchback it was sweet and it was stick shift and I didn't realize it actually only had three cylinders I did not know they made cars only had three cylinders but this one did and what was weird about it is every time that I wanted to get on the interstate I have to turn the air conditioner off and so so I could get enough speed to get up on the interstate and so as you might imagine living in Florida this was not the best sales point for getting a date right so I still got dates. Especially in August right yeah I just didn't tell him to laugh at it but so what happened I took the car for like an oil changer so at the one time and the guys like Aynick did you know that your car is only running on two cylinders well not only did I know they made three cylinder cars I was only running on two out of the three and so I said no I had no idea he said well let me fix it for you I paid the guy fixed it and now I can get on the interstate with air conditioning my dates were happy again life changed everything was great. Bliss after that right the theme music came on all that stuff now so but it's like most of us are running our businesses that way right because we our businesses really need to run on three cylinders media marketing and PR these are all three of the cylinders they've got media marketing and PR and most people are running on one or maybe two of them rarely three of them right and the only way to really grow your businesses to have all three of these things working for you so let me give you an example I have people all the time who will say hey Nick I created this piece of media they don't typically say it that way but I got a business card or I got a brochure I got I made this DVD this movie whatever it is and I got it I mean and I think you know what you're talking about Nick but I've done some stuff like I put out a book but it didn't grow my business at all well that's just the that's just the media that's the media part yeah the media again is just the piece that tells your story just think of it that way okay media is just the piece that tells your story then on top of that you have to have PR PR there's two types of PR there's like pre PR and post PR right so pre PR is really what you people still do it but it's kind of traditional style of PR kind of old style again it still works but it's when something big's about to happen yeah and or something big just happened and you go out to major news outlets and you send out press releases in hopes that you're going to get interviews coverage someone's going to pick it up and and that's good like so when we won the first Emmy I sent out that style of PR to all the like organizations I've been involved with at University of Florida and everywhere I thought might be interested in an alumni or a member you know winning an Emmy right so I did that and I got some pick up but then there's post PR which is really great in this internet era which is writing a press release so press releases first of all are this amazing media format because they're written in third person but you get to write them and you get to approve them and so you get to say whatever you want about yourself in third person now they've got to be honest right but there's not really it's not like a testimonial like most financial advisors for instance can't put out testimonials they can't have testimonials but you can put out press releases so it's just an interesting format that's a testimonial you essentially get to write yourself right third person right right so post PR is taking something that just happened or something that's maybe only interesting in your industry it may even just be to drive traffic and credibility but like for our clients when let's say when they become a best-selling author they get a press release it says they became a best-selling author right we syndicate that out online for them we don't expect any media source to really pick that up right but it gets indicated on hundreds and hundreds of websites and what happens for our clients is when people are searching for them by name or their business name when the first thing that comes up on google is that they are a best-selling author in third person and when we sign up to a publishing deal we do that too and when they get on our tv shows we do that too and so what happens is when you're the first thing you do when you meet somebody new in the marketplace is you google them right right and if you see there's one of several things going to happen if they don't come up at all you wonder if they're in the witness protection program right because there's got like why is there nothing that this can't be right or it might be something might come up that says they won top restaurant in 1987 well that would have been done for me lately yeah exactly so now we're wondering how are you relevant but so the best thing you can have is relevant content near the top yeah we do that through all sorts of media syndication with blogs and articles and press releases and all that but when they call these when our clients just for instance people go to google them they find that they got a publishing deal they were just on tv they were best-selling author and so now the validations are so i use pr the validate right and it's great because it's not me saying hey i just got a publishing deal hey i just it's it's it's a you're again it's written in third person right and now it's on a third party website not your website it's on you know some pr site or whatever it is so really great other credibility telling how great you are versus me saying how great i am because that only goes so far it validates your position in the market but notice i didn't say anywhere that will bring you revenue right i never want so we're still missing revenue we're still in this whole equation marketing is the only thing that will truly drive revenue the biggest mistake i see a lot of people make with pr it's not that it doesn't work it just has its own position for what it does people mistake what it does for what it really does yeah our again validates marketing is you going out into the marketplace and soliciting people to to want to do business with you now if you have that pr to back it up and you have the media to tell your story this game is so much easier to play definitely i've had a tons of people say hey nick i know you do all this pr stuff but i've been ripped off before you know i spent five to ten thousand dollars a month trying to get on tv trying to get newspapers sometimes look pr agents work like interested in what you're selling that person's not they're interested in great content great content for their platform yeah exactly and so people say nick pr doesn't work and say well pr pr does work it works great but it works for giving you relevance and credibility right marketing is what will actually drive revenue so let's talk about the second concept because i think it'll tie it all together yeah so so that first concept again the business trifecta it's media marketing and pr make sure you really understand each of those in your action guide there's a place that breaks those down the second concept that we're talking about here is the secret formula for media success because all media is not created equal correct so let's get in and dive right in so there's really two types of media there's mass media and what we'll call direct media okay mass media television radio newspaper magazines great formats i'll explain it this way so my first big media placement ever was i got in the new york times i got quoted in an article about how to make a small business look bigger and so i was really excited article runs and you know my mom and my friends who i told they're all proud but i literally only got one email about being the article from someone i didn't know who was just saying hey you told me about this yeah good stuff that was true great right yeah and so i got one email from this one guy and his offer to me was to frame me being in the new york times so that was the only solicitation or question or i didn't get any businesses to point to me in the new yorks now the good news is is i bought the frame and now it hangs on my wall in the office because now my with all of our media as people come to office you'll see yeah our walls talk it has all the stuff we've practically ever done and you're just blown away when you walk in you then ask questions but i don't have to say hey did you see me in the new york times hey did you see me in the and the thing about most mass media formats is whether it's good morning america whether it's a magazine placement of forbs or an ink or in usa today or being on the radio it typically runs once and then is gone it's kind of gone yeah depending on a daily newspaper it's gone today right people expecting the next one tomorrow it's not going to do anything for you after that so what people don't understand is that mass media is great for two things credibility and awareness now get your hands on that however you can because you're going to need a archived form of that media right it can be tough when you don't own that media so we have strategies at the agency where we always own our media so we can use it more but that so that's mass media right yeah then direct media is what drives revenue direct media really is the concept of building an audience that actually cares about what you have to say so it could be by collecting business cards you can be by meeting people at trade shows you can be by having an opt-in on your website it's all these things where you're basically collecting a list of people who actually could hire you so for instance we've got a great client who teaches moms how to lose the baby fat so how to lose weight after you've had a baby well there's not a lot of you know 94 year old men who are very interested in their content right they might be watching Good Morning America so she got a good morning America you've got a lot of people who could never hire you right so the concept of direct media is creating a list of people again through opt-ins through meeting people through buying direct mail lists whatever the case is whatever it is lists that are relevant of people who actually could could use your service and may warrant your service yeah and gather that list and then spending your time energy and effort creating your own media to send out to those people so that your own media could be books DVD CDs newsletter sales letters postcards all that sort of stuff right and you then create your own media and send it out to those people really that direct media you could switch that word down for marketing right direct media is really direct marketing you're building a list of relevant people and you're making offers to them through direct media now the only problem with this strategy is you are missing credibility because when you're getting sold you know you're getting sold right you know that well this is cool I got this piece in in the mail but who is this guy who is this person right and so the secret formula for media success is taking mass media credentials and getting the fastest easiest most inexpensive way possible and then inserting them in your direct media so for a facetious example if I showed up on your doorstep you never heard of me before in a sales letter it might start out with something like this it wouldn't actually be all of this but but if it did it would get your attention hi mc nanton you might have seen me recently on NBC CBS ABC or Fox and CNN Fox News ms mbc or cmbc perhaps you've read one of my 33 best-selling books or or maybe you've seen one of my emmy woodwarding films or see me in us today while she's generally magazine anyway you get the point yeah like wow wow this guy must be doing something right who is this guy how come I've never heard of him have I been in a coma that's the question you ask right because you now realize you've missed the boat right so the fact that you've got to get on the train quickly if you want to be relevant in this marketplace so using mass media credentials in your direct media is a secret formula for media success that is what changes the game because you're using media to sell right you're using media in a form as a form of marketing and again we always insert some pr in there too to make sure that people can validate who you say you are now what they're going to do is they're going to go to google and they're going to try to find you and they better be able to find some of this stuff you better have a good web presence all that sort of stuff right but that's kind of those are the two main concepts I think if people can understand those the rest is gravy but you've got to understand the concepts if you don't understand that or you don't understand why you'd want to be out in front of your business yeah you're really missing the those things know you like you trust you that's what a woman do business with you another thing I think that's important is the higher you get up the ladder of affluence with people with money the talk about what we're going to be going through here in this program. Sure we're going to go through the five stages of building a celebrity brand in business the first one being finding your niche or niche depending where you live in the world but that's a huge piece again you don't have to be famous to everybody you need to be a celebrity to the people who actually could hire you and that's what we focus on because that's a tangible amount of people and it's not just luck it's something you can do very methodically right that's the first one. Stage two is all about creating your brand we're going to talk all about how to get there what it looks like and it's not just random stuff that you do it's a step-by-step methodical process for creating your own personal brand right. Love it. In stage three we're going to now take that brand and now develop it into that celebrity expert status so taking the fact that you are the best in your market at what you do and now helping you to become the most known so you can afford to you know command those fees and control your marketplace right. Absolutely and then stage four we're going to talk about once you've built this how to expand it one of the biggest objections I get from people is that they say well Nick if I build the business on the back of me I'm never going to be able to sell it I'm never going to be stuck in the office all the time not true at all you have to be the person standing out front we've actually worked with several businesses where when the business got sold the person who was the brand actually got paid to stay on and be like a celebrity spokesperson and there's lots of ways to transfer your celebrity status your knowledge to other people one time we did a we had a big seminar system and we had multiple teams going out on the road every week and we were worried that we only had one main guy who was on the infomercial was on everything this is the guy well what we did was we we had the guy on everything he would say on the infomercial you know we're coming out to see you and he's talking about the business not and we're coming out to see you right but you took him off the road so you can do what you do best which is creating more media and then when they got there his picture was on the sign his picture was everywhere so they knew in the right place but then someone from the the road team would come up to the front of the room and say hey guys we're about to get started but before we do I want to I want to play a message from our founder and they cue the video the founder then does all this stuff and then starts building credibility and pushing it towards the speaker for the day right so he builds his own credibility or her credibility and then the the the road leader comes back up and says and now would you please help me welcome the stage our speaker for today and whatever that person is right right and so we just just that's a simple example of how we took a guy and took him out of the marketplace and then used him to build credibility for other people who could go out and do his job and and actually the other people actually ended up having a higher closing percentage because he could say things about them and they could say things about him that you can't say about yourself right exactly it's a form of verbal PR we'll call it I love that but so that's so we'll talk about that and how you can why you'd want to roll out how you can roll out and expand in in creating what what JW our partner calls creating legacy dollars I think this is going to be really important because you know at some point we might want to exit the business or we might want to move
